understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through the KA59 puzzle levels by positioning every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with '+', ensuring that in levels with multiple goals all are occupied simultaneously to trigger WIN, within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently controlled piece is the one labeled 'P' and identified by player.id/position; MOVE_* attempts to shift it by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, with success depending on collisions and special boundary behavior. Walls ('#') generally block movement, while blocks ('B') can be pushable when immediately adjacent in the move direction, causing the block to shift with the piece if space allows; movement_blocked is only a hint and actions can still succeed or fail depending on actual interactions. When multiple selectables exist, CLICK switches control to the selectable located at the exact pixel coordinates provided (matching the positions listed in the state/objects), allowing different pieces ('S') to be moved to cover different goals.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the surrounding wall tiles actually block movement; try moving right from the starting pocket.
history.reasoning: Test whether the surrounding wall tiles actually block movement; try moving right from the starting pocket.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether we can move by swapping/digging into adjacent wall cells to create a path toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Test whether we can move by swapping/digging into adjacent wall cells to create a path toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Try pushing the adjacent selectable to the right; sometimes pushes can move through/along tight wall constraints.
history.reasoning: Try pushing the adjacent selectable to the right; sometimes pushes can move through/along tight wall constraints.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | The current selectable is completely enclosed by walls, so switch to the other selectable at [33,21] to see if it can access/push something toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: The current selectable is completely enclosed by walls, so switch to the other selectable at [33,21] to see if it can access/push something toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Try stepping right into the adjacent goal tile; goals are non-collidable so the move may succeed even if flagged blocked.
history.reasoning: Try stepping right into the adjacent goal tile; goals are non-collidable so the move may succeed even if flagged blocked.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_LEFT | Try stepping onto the adjacent goal tile to the left; the 'blocked' hint is likely just detecting overlap with the goal/wall boundary.
history.reasoning: Try stepping onto the adjacent goal tile to the left; the 'blocked' hint is likely just detecting overlap with the goal/wall boundary.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_UP | Try moving up into the goal region; the goal itself is non-collidable so this may place the selectable onto it despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: Try moving up into the goal region; the goal itself is non-collidable so this may place the selectable onto it despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 8: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable so we can try moving it out of the left pocket toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable so we can try moving it out of the left pocket toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 9: CLICK | This selectable is fully boxed in by walls, so switch control to the other piece to move it onto the goal area.
history.reasoning: This selectable is fully boxed in by walls, so switch control to the other piece to move it onto the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_LEFT | Test whether the left wall truly blocks movement or allows slipping/transition through to reach the other selectable’s area.
history.reasoning: Test whether the left wall truly blocks movement or allows slipping/transition through to reach the other selectable’s area.

history.summary: Turn 11: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right into the adjacent goal area; even if flagged blocked, it may allow occupying the goal tile.
history.reasoning: Try moving right into the adjacent goal area; even if flagged blocked, it may allow occupying the goal tile.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_RIGHT | Try shifting right into the goal area so the selectable’s 3x3 fully fits within the 5x5 goal region (overlap may not be enough).
history.reasoning: Try shifting right into the goal area so the selectable’s 3x3 fully fits within the 5x5 goal region (overlap may not be enough).
